steps.title: Identify the implied agent in the opening phrase
steps.content: The phrase “After pleading for years—to no effect—for the passage of key antidiscrimination legislation” contains an action (pleading) with an implied agent: the activists. Standard English requires that the subject of the main clause match this agent to avoid a dangling modifier—a phrase that becomes grammatically disconnected from the sentence.

steps.title: Apply to find the correct subject
steps.content: Choice D places “the activists” as the subject of the main clause, matching the agent from the opening phrase. The other three use different subjects—pressure (A), a protest (B), or lawmakers (C)—that don't match, leaving the opening phrase dangling in each case. That matches choice D — D is correct.
